one_over_dt_scaling_probe.note: lambda ~1/dt (lambda*dt roughly constant) => per-step numerical artifact, not a genuine Lyapunov exponent

integrator: RK4 on smooth-saturated (tanh) forces, vectorised; cells classified by resolution scaling (converged / 1-over-dt artifact / unresolved) at dt = DT, DT/2, DT/4
preregistered_falsifier: a GENUINE (resolution-converged) lambda_max > 0.02 AND recurrence_fraction < 0.5 on a compact set; a lambda that scales as 1/dt is a numerical artifact, not a genuine exponent, and is not a falsifier
verdict: TRICHOTOMY HOLDS FOR THE RESOLVED (SMOOTH) AGENTS; THE HARDEST REGIME REMAINS OPEN. 4/12 cells have a resolution-converged Lyapunov exponent -- all Case-1 convergent (lambda ~ -5 to -23, high recurrence), no falsifier. The other 8/12 strong-recession cells do NOT converge: their apparent positive exponent GROWS as the step shrinks (recession-2 probe: lambda = 121->217->528->722 as dt = DT->DT/8, a ~6-fold rise). That is clearly resolution-DIVERGENT, not a genuine finite exponent. It is CONSISTENT WITH a per-step (1/dt) numerical artifact -- but only roughly: lambda*dt = [2.43, 2.17, 2.64, 1.81] is bounded yet noisy and non-monotonic, not the clean constant a pure artifact would give, so the diagnosis is PRESUMPTIVE, not conclusive. Two readings remain open and this experiment cannot separate them: (a) a numerical artifact from the hard near-discontinuity when a fleeing bump passes through an agent; or (b) a GENUINE quasi-discontinuous reconfiguration of the value landscape -- which is precisely the abrupt value-base mutation Paper 1 wants to model, and for which the smooth-flow Poincare-recurrence premise does not straightforwardly apply. The pre-registration's '1/dt => artifact' rule means E2 cannot, by construction, return a falsifier from this regime. Honest status: Sec 7.5/7.6 is STRENGTHENED for smooth high-dimensional agents; the quasi-discontinuous strong-recession regime -- the one most faithful to metanoia -- is numerically UNRESOLVED and remains genuinely open, not settled either way. NB the one low-recurrence cell that could otherwise have been a falsifier (recession=2.0,diversity=0.0, recurrence < 0.5) is exactly one of these resolution-divergent cells -- so whether it is a falsifier turns entirely on reading (a) vs (b) above, which this run cannot decide.
